mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing present values of future cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes is positive and statistically significant over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns are negative in >55% of periods where both series trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in the industrial production index reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative relationship between INDPRO and forward returns disappears or reverses during tightening periods, or Rank IC is insignificant (p > 0.10) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which increases borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold effect is not statistically distinguishable from noise in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE changes shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01), or forward returns are negative in >55% of divergence periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts reflects strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which correlates with positive economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST trends is negative or insignificant over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >60% of rising housing start periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below its 12-month moving average, it indicates a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO crosses below its 12-month moving average, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample validation.
